summary: Strip the 32-byte SHA-256(host_key) prefix only when it actually matches the expected hash, so older Chrome cookies (no prefix) are not silently truncated.
reason: The 32-byte plaintext prefix is added by Chrome's M120+ cookie host-binding feature. Older Chrome versions write cookies without it, so the unconditional strip discarded the first 32 chars of every JWT-sized Auth0 cookie on older Chrome and produced opaque auth failures. We now verify the prefix matches SHA-256(host_key) before stripping (greptile P2).
